cases.title: TS-17: QR scheduler issues successive tokens at 30 s ± 1 s intervals on real PostgreSQL
preconditions: Active session with QrService scheduler started after SessionService.open
Real PostgreSQL test database running via Compose
TestClock injectable and advanced in controlled 30-second increments
qr_tokens table accessible for issued_at timestamp inspection
steps: Record issued_at of first token at T0 after session open bootstrap
Advance TestClock 30 s and trigger QrService.rotateTokens scheduler tick; record second token issued_at at T1
Advance TestClock 30 s and trigger scheduler tick; record third token issued_at at T2
Assert T1 minus T0 is between 29 and 31 seconds inclusive
Assert T2 minus T1 is between 29 and 31 seconds inclusive
Assert each token has expires_at equal to issued_at plus 30 seconds
cases.expected: Testing-plan §3 TS-17 satisfied: token issuance cadence meets NFR-06 target of new token every 30 s ± 1 s per 07-non-functional-risk §2.2

cases.title: QrService.rotateTokens persists new Valid token and expires previous token at 30 s boundary
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database is running
Active session row exists with status Active
One Valid qr_tokens row exists for sessionId issued at T with expires_at T + 30 s
QrService.rotateTokens wired to QrTokenRepository with TestClock at T + 30 s
steps: Count qr_tokens rows with status Valid for sessionId (expect 1)
Invoke QrService.rotateTokens(sessionId) at TestClock T + 30 s
Query qr_tokens for sessionId ordered by issued_at DESC
Assert newest row has status Valid with expires_at minus issued_at equal to 30 seconds
Assert previous token row status is Expired
Assert interval between consecutive issued_at timestamps is between 29 and 31 seconds
cases.expected: Module boundary enforces NFR-06 rotation cadence: one Valid token at a time; previous Valid → Expired at each 30 s cycle per SM-03 §4.4

cases.title: SessionService.open bootstrap starts QR scheduler and issues first token within 1 s of openedAt
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database running
Draft session with valid room GPS configured
SessionService.open orchestrates QrService.startScheduler per SM-01 §6.1
TestClock fixed at session open instant T
steps: Invoke SessionService.open(sessionId) at T
Query qr_tokens for sessionId ordered by issued_at ASC
Assert first Valid token exists with issued_at within 1 second of session openedAt
Assert first token expires_at equals issued_at plus 30 seconds
Verify QrService scheduler registered for sessionId
cases.expected: QR issuance begins immediately on session open; first token available within NFR-06 ± 1 s tolerance of Active transition per FR-05 side effect

cases.title: Five consecutive rotation cycles maintain 30 s ± 1 s cadence without scheduler drift
preconditions: Active session with QrService scheduler running in test environment
TestClock advances in 30-second increments across five cycles
qr_tokens table records all issued_at timestamps
steps: Record issued_at timestamps T0 through T5 across five rotateTokens invocations
Compute delta between each consecutive pair (T1-T0, T2-T1, T3-T2, T4-T3, T5-T4)
Assert every delta is between 29 and 31 seconds inclusive
Assert cumulative drift from T0 to T5 is at most 5 seconds (5 cycles × ±1 s tolerance)
Assert exactly one Valid token exists after each rotation
cases.expected: NFR-06 verification method satisfied: automated test of token issue timestamps shows stable 30 s ± 1 s cadence over extended Active session

cases.title: GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current returns secondsRemaining accurate to ± 1 s of expiresAt
preconditions: Instructor session cookie is set
Active sessionId with QR scheduler running and Valid token just issued at T
TestClock or server time synchronized with test harness
steps: Send GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current with Instructor session at T + 5 s
Assert HTTP status 200
Assert response body contains issuedAt, expiresAt, and secondsRemaining
Compute expected secondsRemaining as floor((expiresAt - now) in seconds)
Assert abs(secondsRemaining - expected) ≤ 1
Assert secondsRemaining is between 24 and 26 at T + 5 s (30 s TTL minus 5 s elapsed ± 1 s)
cases.expected: HTTP 200 QrTokenDisplay contract delivers countdown accurate ± 1 s per NFR-06 instructor display countdown accuracy target

cases.title: Consecutive GET /qr/current polls detect token rotation after 30 s with new tokenId
preconditions: Instructor session cookie is set
Active session with QR scheduler running
TestClock or wall-clock timing controlled for 30-second wait
steps: GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current at T and capture tokenId1, issuedAt1, secondsRemaining1
Assert secondsRemaining1 is between 29 and 30 inclusive immediately after issuance
Wait 30 seconds (or advance TestClock 30 s and trigger scheduler)
GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current at T + 30 s and capture tokenId2, issuedAt2, secondsRemaining2
Assert tokenId2 differs from tokenId1
Assert issuedAt2 minus issuedAt1 is between 29 and 31 seconds
Assert secondsRemaining2 is between 29 and 30 inclusive
cases.expected: API polling confirms NFR-06 rotation: new token every 30 s ± 1 s with fresh countdown per AC-06a and testing-plan TS-17

cases.title: QrToken scheduler tick transitions previous Valid to Expired and issues new Valid at 30 s mark
preconditions: Valid qr_tokens row in PostgreSQL with issued_at at T and expires_at at T + 30 s
QrService.rotateTokens callable at TestClock T + 30 s
Active session parent row exists
steps: Record beforeState: one Valid token with tokenId1
Invoke QrService.rotateTokens(sessionId) at T + 30 s
Query qr_tokens for tokenId1 and assert afterState Expired
Query newest qr_tokens row and assert status Valid with fresh 30 s window
Assert issued_at of new token is between T + 29 s and T + 31 s
cases.expected: SM-03 rotation sequence per §4.4: previous Valid → Expired; new Valid issued at NFR-06 cadence boundary

cases.title: Concurrent rotateTokens calls at scheduler tick yield single new Valid token without cadence disruption
preconditions: Active session in PostgreSQL with one Valid qr_tokens row issued at T
QrService.rotateTokens invocable concurrently from parallel test workers
TestClock fixed at T + 30 s
steps: Launch two parallel QrService.rotateTokens(sessionId) calls at T + 30 s
Await both operations to settle
Count qr_tokens rows with status Valid for sessionId
Assert exactly one Valid token exists
Assert previous Valid token is Expired
Assert new token issued_at is between T + 29 s and T + 31 s
cases.expected: NFR-06 cadence preserved under concurrent scheduler triggers; no duplicate Valid tokens or skipped rotation cycle

cases.title: NFR-06 gate fails when measured issuance interval falls outside 29–31 second tolerance
preconditions: Active session with QrService scheduler instrumented for timestamp capture
Test harness capable of detecting cadence violations
At least two consecutive token issued_at timestamps recorded
steps: Record consecutive token issuance timestamps T0 and T1 from qr_tokens table
Compute interval delta = T1 minus T0 in seconds
If delta < 29 or delta > 31, mark NFR-06 verification FAIL and record remediation
If delta between 29 and 31 inclusive, mark NFR-06 verification PASS for that cycle
Repeat across minimum 3 rotation cycles for pilot gate evidence
cases.expected: NFR-06 pass/fail boundary enforced at 30 s ± 1 s; intervals outside 29–31 s block pilot sign-off per 07-non-functional-risk §2.2 verification

cases.title: TS-18: Expired token rejected at T + 31 s confirms NFR-06 expiry enforcement per BR-03
preconditions: Active session with enrolled Student authenticated
QrToken issued at time T with status Valid
TestClock fixed at T + 31 seconds
Student coordinates within session gpsRadiusMeters
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in with tokenId from token issued at T using Student session
Assert HTTP status 400 with outcome ExpiredQr and errorCode ExpiredQr
Assert response message is Mã QR đã hết hạn, vui lòng quét mã mới
Verify attendance remains Pending
Confirm token age exceeds 30 s absolute window per NFR-06 expired-token rejection target
cases.expected: Testing-plan §3 TS-18 satisfied: NFR-06 requires expired tokens rejected per BR-03; 31 s scan fails with ExpiredQr confirming 30 s hard window

cases.title: Flow A step 3: QR poll returns token with secondsRemaining within NFR-06 30 s validity window
preconditions: Instructor opened Draft session to Active (Flow A steps 1–2 complete)
Authenticated Instructor session available
QR scheduler started on session open
steps: Poll GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current every 5 seconds with Instructor session until HTTP 200
Capture tokenId, issuedAt, expiresAt, and secondsRemaining from first successful response
Assert secondsRemaining is between 1 and 30 inclusive
Assert expiresAt minus issuedAt equals 30 seconds
Pass tokenId to student check-in within captured secondsRemaining window
cases.expected: Flow A QR polling delivers token within NFR-06 30 s window with accurate countdown for timely student scan per testing-plan §6 Flow A

cases.title: Student denied GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current — countdown endpoint restricted to Instructor
preconditions: Authenticated Student session is available
Active sessionId with valid rotating QR token
Student role lacks qr:display permission per roles-permissions §2.3
steps: GET /api/v1/sessions/:sessionId/qr/current using Student session cookie
Assert HTTP status 403 Forbidden
Assert response errorCode Forbidden with localized permission-denied message
Verify no qrPayload, tokenId, or secondsRemaining returned in response body
cases.expected: QR countdown and rotation data accessible only to Instructor with qr:display; Student cannot observe token refresh cadence via API

cases.title: Instructor QR display countdown decrements each second and refreshes within ± 1 s of 30 s cycle
preconditions: Instructor is logged in to instructor SPA
Active session exists for assigned class HESD-01
Instructor navigates to session detail QR tab with QrCountdown visible
Displayed QR token rotates via scheduler cadence (protected preview fixture IDs excluded from instructor display per NFR-06)
steps: Verify QrCountdown shows seconds remaining (Mã mới sau X giây) between 1 and 30 on load
Observe countdown decrement by 1 each second for at least 5 consecutive seconds
Record browser timestamp when countdown shows 1 second remaining
Wait for QR image or token payload to change (rotation event)
Assert elapsed time from countdown-at-1 to QR refresh is between 0 and 2 seconds
Assert countdown resets to approximately 30 seconds (29–30 inclusive) after refresh
cases.expected: Instructor display countdown accuracy meets NFR-06 ± 1 s target; auto-refresh every 30 s per AC-06a and manual projector display check verification method

cases.title: Fullscreen QR presentation at 1280×720 rotates every 30 s with visible countdown timer
preconditions: Instructor is logged in with Active session in progress
Browser viewport set to 1280×720 per device matrix and NFR-20 field test spec
Instructor navigates to /sessions/:id/qr-present fullscreen mode
Displayed token rotates on 30 s scheduler cadence (not static preview fixture token)
steps: Verify no instructor sidebar or nav chrome obscures QR in fullscreen presentation per production UI quality bar §5.2
Verify large QR code renders on --color-qr-bg (#000000) with --color-qr-fg (#ffffff) modules per design tokens §3.5
Verify countdown timer using --color-qr-countdown (#ffffff) on black background is visible and updating each second
Record timestamp T_start when countdown shows 30
Wait one full rotation cycle until QR image updates
Assert elapsed time T_rotate minus T_start is between 29 and 31 seconds
Verify countdown resets to approximately 30 after rotation without full page reload
cases.expected: Projector display check per NFR-06 verification: 30 s ± 1 s rotation with visible countdown at 1280×720; high-contrast QR mode and no chrome obscuring QR per production UI quality bar §2 and §5.2

cases.title: QR countdown warning accent switches to --color-qr-warning at ≤ 10 s remaining
preconditions: Instructor is logged in with Active session QR tab open
QrCountdown component visible with countdown above 10 seconds
Design tokens §3.5 define --color-qr-accent (#22c55e) for > 10 s and --color-qr-warning (#facc15) for ≤ 10 s
steps: Observe QrCountdown text color uses --color-qr-accent (#22c55e) when secondsRemaining > 10
Wait until countdown reaches 10 seconds or below
Verify countdown text color switches to --color-qr-warning (#facc15)
Verify countdown continues decrementing each second through warning state
Verify QR rotates and countdown resets to accent color after 30 s cycle completes
cases.expected: Countdown visual state aligns with remaining validity window; warning at ≤ 10 s gives instructor and students timely refresh cue per design tokens and NFR-06 timeliness

cases.title: Student receives ExpiredQr after instructor display rotates away from scanned QR
preconditions: Instructor Active session with QR display rotating every 30 s
Authenticated Student on mobile viewport 375px with GPS permission granted
Student captures deep link with tokenId from current QR display
steps: Student opens check-in deep link with tokenId captured from instructor display
Wait until instructor QR display completes one 30 s rotation cycle (countdown reaches 0 and QR changes)
Student submits check-in after GPS capture with stale tokenId
Verify error panel displays Mã QR đã hết hạn, vui lòng quét mã mới
Verify Quét lại action returns student to scanner for fresh QR within new 30 s window
cases.expected: End-to-end NFR-06 enforcement: UI rotation invalidates stale codes; expired tokens rejected with Vietnamese message per BR-03 and AC-06b

cases.title: QrService.stopScheduler on session close halts token issuance immediately
preconditions: Active session with QrService scheduler running and Valid token issued at T
SessionService.close orchestrates QrService.stopScheduler per SM-01 §6.2
Real PostgreSQL test database
steps: Record qr_tokens count and newest issued_at before close
Invoke SessionService.close(sessionId) at T + 15 s
Advance TestClock to T + 45 s (past next scheduled rotation)
Query qr_tokens for sessionId and assert no new Valid tokens created after close
Assert session status is Closed and scheduler no longer registered
cases.expected: Token refresh cadence stops on session close; no spurious issuances after Active ends preserving NFR-06 scope to live check-in window only

cases.title: Check-in at T + 29 s succeeds; at T + 31 s returns ExpiredQr confirming 30 s absolute window
preconditions: Active session with two enrolled Students authenticated
QrToken issued at T for first check-in; fresh token at T2 for second
Both students have in-radius coordinates and Pending attendance
TestClock controllable to T + 29 s and T2 + 31 s
steps: Set TestClock to T + 29 seconds and POST /api/v1/check-in with token from T using Student A session
Assert HTTP 200 with outcome Success (within 30 s window)
Set TestClock to T2 + 31 seconds and POST /api/v1/check-in with token from T2 using Student B session
Assert HTTP 400 with outcome ExpiredQr
Verify interval between token issuance and rejection aligns with NFR-06 30 s ± 1 s expiry boundary
cases.expected: NFR-06 and BR-03 boundary: token valid at 29 s, rejected at 31 s; no exceptions to 30-second absolute validity window

cases.title: TS-19: GET /sessions/:sessionId/qr/current when session not Active returns no valid token
preconditions: Draft session exists (status Draft) with valid GPS configured
Closed session exists (status Closed) from prior workshop
Authenticated Instructor with assignment to both sessions
steps: GET /api/v1/sessions/:draftSessionId/qr/current with Instructor session
Assert HTTP 403 with errorCode SessionNotActive and no qrPayload or tokenId in response body
GET /api/v1/sessions/:closedSessionId/qr/current with Instructor session
Assert same session-not-active rejection with no valid rotating token
Query qr_tokens table for both sessionIds and assert no Valid token rows exist
cases.expected: Testing-plan §3 TS-19 satisfied: NFR-06 cadence applies only while Active; no valid QR token issued when session is Draft or Closed per AC-06c and SM-03 §4.5

cases.title: Instructor QR tab shows session-not-active state when session is Draft or Closed
preconditions: Instructor is logged in to instructor SPA
Draft session with GPS configured exists (not yet opened)
Closed session from prior workshop exists
steps: Navigate to QR tab on Draft session detail page
Verify illustration and message Buổi học chưa mở displayed per ui-states §7.2
Verify no QR image or QrCountdown rendered and no successful poll to /api/v1/sessions/:id/qr/current
Navigate to Closed session QR tab and verify session-not-active illustration with no rotating countdown
Confirm Trình chiếu QR action is disabled or absent until session is Active
cases.expected: AC-06c UI state: no valid QR token or NFR-06 countdown displayed when session not Active; instructor sees Buổi học chưa mở placeholder instead of rotation UI
